args.content:
`AdminSagas.tsx` calls two backend endpoints:
1. `POST /api/admin/sagas/:id/refresh` — refreshes saga from TMDB, optionally auto-imports missing movies (existed in admin.ts)
2. `POST /api/admin/import-by-tmdb-ids` — imports movies/series directly by TMDB IDs (added 2026-05-07 via `importMovie`/`importSeries` from auto-import.ts)
Both are in `artifacts/api-server/src/routes/admin.ts`.

args.message:
fix: add missing import-by-tmdb-ids endpoint for saga import buttons

AdminSagas.tsx calls importByTmdbIds() which POSTs to /api/admin/import-by-tmdb-ids,
but the backend endpoint was missing. Only the frontend api.ts function existed.

The individual "Importar" and "Importar todo" buttons on the saga manager
were broken because of this missing endpoint. The "Sincronizar" buttons
worked fine since /api/admin/sagas/:id/refresh already existed.

args.message:
fix+feat: remove duplicate import-by-tmdb-ids route, add cv_sagas table, redesign AdminSagas

PART 1 — Bug fix:
- Removed the duplicate (first) definition of router.post("/admin/import-by-tmdb-ids", ...)
  in admin.ts that was shadowing the correct implementation.
  The first version (lines 459-478) had a basic counter while the second
  (line 820+) had per-item status tracking. Express only executes the first
  matching route, so bulk-importing saga movies was silently failing.

PART 2 — Custom sagas + AdminSagas redesign:
Backend:
- Created cv_sagas table with initSagasTable() seeded from CURATED_COLLECTION_IDS
- Updated GET /api/sagas to read collection_ids from cv_sagas instead of hardcoded list
- Added GET/POST/DELETE /api/admin/sagas endpoints for managing custom sagas
  (POST requires collection_id, DELETE forbidden for curated sagas)
- Registered initSagasTable in index.ts startup chain

Frontend api.ts:
- Added CvSagaRow interface
- Added adminFetchSagas, adminAddSaga, adminDeleteSaga functions

Frontend AdminSagas.tsx — full redesign:
- Left panel: search filtering, sort toggle (by count / alphabetical), poster thumbnails,
  lock icon for curated sagas, trash button for custom sagas, inline add-saga form
- Center panel: blurred backdrop hero, action buttons (Sincronizar, Sincronizar e importar,
  Importar pendientes), status banners, movie grid
- lucide-react icons, sonner toasts, loading spinners throughout

args.message:
fix: sagas — cast param, ON CONFLICT update tmdb_id, include custom sagas in public API

- Cast $1::int[] in findLocalMoviesByTmdbIds to fix type mismatch
- Expand ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET in importMovie/importSeries
  to also set tmdb_id via COALESCE when previously NULL
- Replace empty-response guard in GET /api/sagas with fallback to
  CURATED_COLLECTION_IDS, so custom sagas from cv_sagas appear

args.message:
fix: add missing tmdb_id column to movies table, fix VALUES param count mismatch

Root cause: the movies table was created in the initial schema without a
tmdb_id column, and no ALTER TABLE migration ever added it. This caused
importMovie to silently fail (try/catch swallowed the error), leaving
movies with tmdb_id = NULL even after "importing" — showing "No disponible".

Fix 1: Add ALTER TABLE movies ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tmdb_id INTEGER
to the migration section in db.ts, alongside a redundant but safe
ALTER TABLE cv_series ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tmdb_id INTEGER.

Fix 2: Both importMovie and importSeries had a pre-existing parameter
count mismatch: 27 columns but VALUES ($1..$29) — 29 placeholders.
Reduced to $1..$27 to match the actual column/value count.
